info.title: Java Spring Boot REST API - Health Status Endpoint
info.description: Health and System Resource Status Endpoint for monitoring application uptime and JVM memory metrics.

get.description: Returns application uptime, JVM memory metrics (total allocated and currently free), and server timestamp. No authentication required. Public endpoint suitable for load balancers and monitoring systems.

healthy.summary: Healthy application with good memory availability

low_memory.summary: Application with low free memory (may indicate pressure)

degraded.summary: Memory metrics unavailable (rare; memory access denied)

405.description: Method Not Allowed (invalid HTTP method: POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)

get.x-response-time-note: 99th percentile response time must be under 10ms

HealthStatusResponse.title: HealthStatusResponse
HealthStatusResponse.description: Immutable response object representing application health and system resource status at a point in time.

uptime.description: Application uptime in human-readable format with pattern XhYmZs. Zero components are omitted (e.g., '2h 15m 30s', '45m 30s', '15s'). Updated continuously as application runs; increases monotonically with application runtime.

totalMemoryMB.description: Total JVM memory allocated to the application in megabytes. Obtained from Runtime.getRuntime().totalMemory() converted to MB. Represents current heap allocation, not maximum (-Xmx). If memory metrics cannot be retrieved (rare), value is 0.

freeMemoryMB.description: Currently available JVM free heap memory in megabytes. Obtained from Runtime.getRuntime().freeMemory() converted to MB. Represents space available for new object allocation before garbage collection occurs. Constraint: freeMemoryMB <= totalMemoryMB. Highly variable; changes with garbage collection and allocation. If memory metrics cannot be retrieved (rare), value is 0.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp indicating when this response was generated on the server. Always UTC (ends with Z); never client's local time. Useful for audit trails and correlating metrics with application logs. Precision: seconds (no milliseconds).

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response returned by Spring MVC for invalid HTTP methods and other errors.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Health and System Resource Status Endpoint
